question: [File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getTransactionTimestampsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a transaction_type filter that causes transactionTypeQuery to be applied to the EntityTransaction subquery via the alias replacement `.replace(/(ctl|t)\./g, '')`, incorrectly filtering entity transactions by type and excluding staking reward transactions from the timestamp results?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getTransactionsByTransactionIdsSql()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a transaction ID where the payer account ID is the staking reward account (0.0.800) itself, causing the query to look up transactions paid by account 800, and if such transactions exist, their staking_reward_transfers could be incorrectly attributed to the queried transaction?
